title: Question of Liu and Wood (Theorem 3)
statement_text: Is it true that planar graphs in which no two vertices have many common neighbors are $3$-colorable with bounded clustering?
context_text: Esperet and Joret proved that planar graphs with bounded maximum degree are $3$-colorable with bounded clustering. Liu and Wood asked whether the same conclusion holds when the bounded-maximum-degree assumption is replaced by the weaker condition that no two vertices share many common neighbors.
paper_contribution: Answers the question affirmatively in a stronger form: for any surface $\Sigma$, a subgraph-closed class of graphs drawn on $\Sigma$ is $3$-choosable with bounded clustering if and only if there exists $t$ such that $P''_t$ (the complete join of two isolated vertices with a path on $t$ vertices) does not belong to the class.
notes: The question is attributed to Liu and Wood [10] (arXiv:1905.08969, 2019); its exact statement is reconstructed from the abstract since the full body of Theorem 3 was not provided in the input.
paper_title: Clustered coloring of (path+2K_1)-free graphs on surfaces
